🌴 IsleLearn English X • Fase E

Chapter 2
Personal Recount Text

Telling Personal Experiences — belajar menceritakan pengalaman pribadi secara runtut, jelas, dan bermakna dengan simple past tense, time connectives, action verbs, feelings, dan reflection.

TemaMenceritakan pengalaman pribadi
KelasX / Fase E
LevelBeginner–Elementary
OutputWrite & tell a personal recount
HookObjectivesConceptStructureLanguageVocabularyListeningSpeakingReadingViewingGrammar LabPracticeQuizWritingPresentingFinal TaskRubric
1
Situational Hook

Setiap siswa punya pengalaman yang bisa diceritakan

Mungkin kamu pernah terlambat ke sekolah karena hujan atau rob, ikut orang tua memasang tali rumput laut, pergi memancing pada malam hari, mengikuti lomba antar kelas, naik kapal ke Makassar, membantu keluarga di rumah, mengalami hari pertama masuk SMA, menghadiri acara sekolah, atau mengalami kejadian lucu, menegangkan, dan berkesan.

Warm-up Questions
  • What happened to you yesterday?
  • Where did you go last holiday?
  • What did you do last weekend?
  • What was your most memorable experience?

Dalam bahasa Inggris, pengalaman seperti itu bisa ditulis dalam bentuk personal recount text.

2
Learning Objectives

Setelah mempelajari bab ini, siswa mampu...

  • memahami isi teks recount sederhana tentang pengalaman pribadi;
  • mengidentifikasi urutan kejadian dalam teks recount;
  • menemukan informasi penting: siapa, kapan, di mana, apa yang terjadi, dan bagaimana perasaan penulis;
  • menggunakan simple past tense untuk menceritakan pengalaman masa lalu;
  • menggunakan time connectives seperti first, then, after that, finally;
  • menggunakan action verbs seperti went, helped, saw, joined, visited;
  • menceritakan pengalaman pribadi secara lisan;
  • menulis personal recount text dengan struktur yang benar;
  • memperbaiki tulisan sendiri melalui checklist;
  • mempresentasikan pengalaman pribadi secara singkat dan jelas.
3
Definition & Social Function

What Is Personal Recount Text?

Personal recount text adalah teks yang menceritakan pengalaman pribadi penulis yang terjadi di masa lalu. Tujuan utamanya adalah: to retell past experiences in chronological order.

TopicExample
School experienceMy First Day at Senior High School
Family experienceHelping My Mother at Home
Island life experienceHelping My Father with Seaweed
Travel experienceMy Trip to Makassar
Funny experienceA Funny Moment in Class
Unforgettable experienceThe Day I Got Caught in the Rain
Competition experienceJoining a Class Competition
Daily life experienceA Busy Sunday Morning
SituationRecount Use
Menulis diaryMenceritakan kejadian hari itu
Bercerita kepada temanMenceritakan pengalaman lucu atau menarik
Tugas Bahasa InggrisMenulis pengalaman liburan
Caption media sosialMenceritakan kegiatan yang sudah terjadi
Presentasi kelasMenceritakan pengalaman berkesan
4
Generic Structure

Orientation → Series of Events → Reorientation

Orientation

Mengenalkan siapa, kapan, dan di mana kejadian terjadi.

Example: Last Sunday, I went to the beach with my friends.

Series of Events

Menceritakan kejadian secara berurutan.

Example: First, we walked to the beach. Then, we played near the water.

Reorientation

Menutup cerita dengan perasaan atau kesan pribadi.

Example: It was a simple but unforgettable day.

Orientation
Who? When? Where?
Events
What happened first?
What happened next?
What happened after that?
Reorientation
How did you feel?
What did you learn?
Was it memorable?
5
Language Features

Simple Past, Pronouns, Connectives, and Adverbs

A. Simple Past Tense

Recount text menggunakan simple past tense karena menceritakan kejadian yang sudah terjadi.

Pattern 1: Subject + Verb 2 + Object/Complement

  • I went to school early.
  • We played football after class.
  • She helped her mother.
  • They visited their grandmother.
  • He caught a fish.

B. Was / Were

SubjectPast To BeExample
I / He / She / ItwasI was happy.
You / We / TheywereWe were tired.
  • I was nervous on my first day.
  • The weather was hot.
  • We were very tired.
  • My friends were excited.
Verb 1Verb 2Meaning
playplayedbermain
helphelpedmembantu
visitvisitedmengunjungi
watchwatchedmenonton
cleancleanedmembersihkan
joinjoinedmengikuti
walkwalkedberjalan
arrivearrivedtiba
startstartedmemulai
finishfinishedmenyelesaikan
Verb 1Verb 2Meaning
gowentpergi
comecamedatang
seesawmelihat
eatatemakan
drinkdrankminum
buyboughtmembeli
bringbroughtmembawa
taketookmengambil/membawa
makemademembuat
meetmetbertemu
getgotmendapatkan
catchcaughtmenangkap
findfoundmenemukan
feelfeltmerasa
hearheardmendengar
saysaidmengatakan
sitsatduduk
runranberlari
swimswamberenang
writewrotemenulis

Personal Pronouns & Adverbs of Manner

PronounUse
Isaya
Wekami/kita
Mymilik saya
Ourmilik kami/kita
Mesaya sebagai objek
Uskami/kita sebagai objek
slowlycarefullyhappilyquicklyquietlyloudlyseriouslypatientlysafely
6
Vocabulary Bank

School, Daily Life, Island Life, Feelings, and Sentence Starters

School ActivitiesIndonesian
attended the classmengikuti pelajaran
joined the ceremonymengikuti upacara

submitted homeworkmengumpulkan PR

amazedkagum

Useful Sentence Starters

FunctionSentence Starter
Starting the storyLast Sunday, I ...
Giving placeIt happened in ...
Giving timeIn the morning, ...
First eventFirst, I ...
Next eventThen, I ...
More eventAfter that, I ...
Final eventFinally, I ...
FeelingI felt ...
ClosingIt was a ... experience.
LessonI learned that ...
7
Listening Section

Listen, Understand Main Idea, Details, and Inference

Listening Text 1 — “My First Day at Senior High School”

Last year, I had my first day at senior high school. I woke up earlier than usual because I felt nervous. After taking a bath, I wore my new uniform and prepared my books. My mother reminded me to be brave and polite.

When I arrived at school, I saw many new students in the yard. Some of them talked with their friends, while others stood quietly. At first, I felt shy because I did not know many people. Then, one student smiled and asked my name. We talked for a few minutes and walked to the classroom together.

The teacher welcomed us warmly. She explained the school rules and asked us to introduce ourselves. I was nervous when I stood in front of the class, but I tried to speak clearly. After school, I felt relieved and happy. My first day was not scary. It was memorable.

Listening Tasks

A. Main Idea: What is the text mainly about?

  1. When did the experience happen?
  2. Why did the writer wake up earlier than usual?
  3. What did the writer see in the school yard?
  4. Who helped the writer feel less shy?
  5. How did the writer feel after school?

C. Inference: What does “My first day was not scary” mean?

Listening Text 2 — “Helping My Father with Seaweed”

Last Saturday afternoon, I helped my father with seaweed near the beach. The weather was hot, but the wind was fresh. My father brought several ropes, and I carried a small basket of seaweed seedlings.

First, we sat near the shore and prepared the ropes. Then, my father showed me how to tie the seedlings carefully. At first, I did it slowly because I was afraid of making mistakes. After some practice, I became more confident.

We worked for almost two hours. My hands felt tired, but I was happy because I could help my family. When we finished, my father smiled and thanked me. That afternoon, I learned that simple work can teach responsibility.

  1. What did the writer do last Saturday afternoon?
  2. Where did the activity happen?
  3. What did the father bring?
  4. Why did the writer work slowly at first?
  5. What lesson did the writer learn?
8
Speaking Section

Ask About Past Experiences and Tell Your Own Story

QuestionAnswer
What did you do yesterday?I helped my mother at home.
Where did you go last Sunday?I went to the beach.
Who did you go with?I went with my friends.
What happened after that?After that, we played football.
How did you feel?I felt happy and tired.
Was it memorable?Yes, it was very memorable.

Dialogue 1 — A School Experience

Alya: What did you do yesterday?
Rafi: I joined an English practice in class.
Alya: What did you do there?
Rafi: First, we listened to a short dialogue. Then, we practiced speaking with a partner.
Alya: Was it difficult?
Rafi: Yes, a little. I was nervous at first.
Alya: How did you feel after the practice?
Rafi: I felt happy because I could speak better than before.

Dialogue 2 — An Island Experience

Dina: Did you help your parents last weekend?
Salim: Yes, I did. I helped my father prepare the seaweed ropes.
Dina: Where did you do it?
Salim: We did it near the beach.
Dina: Was it tiring?
Salim: Yes, it was tiring, but I was proud.
Dina: Why?
Salim: Because I helped my family and learned something useful.

Speaking Mini Task

Tell your partner about one experience. Use this frame: Last …, I … / I went there with … / First, … / Then, … / After that, … / Finally, … / I felt … because …

My first day at SMAHelping my parentsA trip to the beachA funny moment in classA day when I was lateMy weekend activityJoining a school eventHelping with seaweed workGoing fishingVisiting family
9
Reading Section

Short, Medium, and Longer Recount Texts

Reading Text 1 — Short: “A Rainy Morning”

Yesterday morning, I went to school in the rain. I woke up late because I forgot to set my alarm. I took a bath quickly and wore my uniform. My mother told me to bring an umbrella, but I forgot it.

On the way to school, the rain became heavier. My shoes were wet, and my bag was a little dirty. When I arrived at school, the first lesson had already started. I felt embarrassed, but my teacher allowed me to enter the class.

It was not a good morning, but I learned to prepare my things before sleeping.

  1. When did the experience happen?
  2. Why did the writer wake up late?
  3. What did the writer forget to bring?
  4. How did the writer feel when arriving at school?
  5. What lesson did the writer learn?

Reading Text 2 — Medium: “My First English Presentation”

Last month, I had my first English presentation in class. The topic was “My Favorite Place.” I chose the beach near my house because it was easy for me to describe.

Before the presentation, I felt very nervous. I wrote my text at home and practiced it several times. My sister listened to me and helped me correct some words. On the presentation day, I stood in front of the class with shaking hands.

First, I greeted my friends. Then, I introduced my topic. I described the beach, the blue water, the small boats, and the fresh wind. I made some pronunciation mistakes, but my friends listened carefully. My teacher smiled and gave me helpful feedback.

After the presentation, I felt proud. I realized that speaking English was difficult, but practice made it easier. It was a simple presentation, but it gave me more confidence.

  1. What was the presentation topic?
  2. Why did the writer choose the beach?
  3. Who helped the writer practice?
  4. What did the writer describe?
  5. What did the writer realize after the presentation?
  6. Mention three past verbs from the text.

Reading Text 3 — Longer: “A Memorable Trip to Makassar”

Last holiday, my family and I went to Makassar. It was my first trip to the city after several months on the island. I felt excited because I wanted to see tall buildings, busy roads, and large stores.

We left Sapuka by boat early in the morning. The sea was calm at first, but after two hours, the waves became bigger. I felt a little afraid, so I sat near my mother and held my bag tightly. My father told me to stay calm. After several hours, we arrived safely at the harbor.

In Makassar, we visited my uncle’s house. The neighborhood was very different from my village. There were many vehicles, bright lights, and crowded streets. The next day, my uncle took us to a bookstore. I was amazed because I saw many English books, dictionaries, and school supplies. I bought one small notebook and a pen.

On the last day, we visited Losari Beach. The place was crowded, but the view was beautiful. We took some photos and ate traditional snacks. I felt happy, but I also missed the quiet atmosphere of Sapuka.

The trip taught me that every place has its own beauty. Makassar was busy and modern, while Sapuka was quiet and peaceful. I was thankful because I could experience both places.

  1. When did the writer go to Makassar?
  2. How did the writer travel from Sapuka?
  3. Why did the writer feel afraid on the boat?
  4. What did the writer see in the bookstore?
  5. What place did the family visit on the last day?
  6. How did the writer compare Makassar and Sapuka?
  7. What lesson did the writer learn?
10
Viewing Section

Read the Picture Story Before Writing

Guru dapat menggunakan gambar berseri, foto kegiatan sekolah, atau foto aktivitas sehari-hari agar siswa memahami urutan kejadian.

Viewing Questions

  1. What happened first?
  2. What happened next?
  3. What did the student do after school?
  4. How did the student feel?
  5. What sentence can describe the last picture?

Sentence Frames

  • First, he/she …
  • Then, he/she …
  • After that, he/she …
  • In the afternoon, he/she …
  • At night, he/she …
  • Finally, he/she felt …
11
Grammar Lab

Verb 1 → Verb 2, Complete Sentences, Arrange Events

A. Change the Verbs

go → went
help → helped
see → saw
play → played
eat → ate
bring → brought
study → studied
walk → walked
feel → felt
write → wrote

B. Complete the Sentences

  1. Yesterday, I ___ to school early.
  2. Last night, we ___ English together.
  3. My father ___ a fishing net.
  4. I ___ very nervous before the presentation.
  5. They ___ football after class.

Word bank: played — studied — went — prepared — felt

Answers: went, studied, prepared, felt, played

C. Arrange the Events

  1. Finally, we went home happily.
  2. Last Sunday, my friends and I went to the beach.
  3. After that, we sat under a tree and ate snacks.
  4. First, we walked from our houses to the beach.
  5. Then, we played near the water.

Correct order: 2 → 4 → 5 → 3 → 1

12
Common Mistakes

Kesalahan yang Sering Muncul

MistakeCorrectionExplanation
Yesterday I go to school.Yesterday I went to school.Recount memakai Verb 2.
I was go to the beach.I went to the beach.Jangan gabung was + Verb 1.
We was happy.We were happy.We memakai were.
She help her mother yesterday.She helped her mother yesterday.Help menjadi helped.
I feel nervous last week.I felt nervous last week.Feel menjadi felt.
Then I am happy.Then I was happy.Am menjadi was.
13
Guided Practice

Complete, Write from Pictures, and Expand Sentences

Practice 1 — Complete the Recount

Last Sunday, I ___ my mother at home. First, I cleaned the ___. Then, I washed the ___. After that, I helped her cook rice. I felt ___ because I could help my family.

Word bank: dishes — happy — helped — kitchen

Practice 2 — Write Sentences from Pictures

Look at the picture story and write five sentences. Use: first, then, after that, finally, felt.

Example: First, the student woke up early. Then, he went to school. After that, he helped his father. Finally, he studied at night. He felt tired but happy.

Practice 3 — Expand the Sentence

I went to school. → Yesterday morning, I went to school early with my friend.

I helped my father. → Last Saturday afternoon, I helped my father tie seaweed ropes near the beach.

I was happy. → I was happy because I finished my first English presentation.

We played football. → After class, we played football in the school yard for one hour.

14
Interactive Quiz

Check Your Understanding

Question 1. What is the purpose of personal recount text?
Answer: B
Question 2. Which tense is commonly used in recount text?
Answer: B
Question 3. Which one is a time connective?
Answer: B
Question 4. Which sentence is correct?
Answer: B
Question 5. Which part introduces who, when, and where?
Answer: A
15
Writing Section

Write a Personal Recount Text

A. Writing Mission

Write a personal recount text about one experience. Choose one topic:

My First Day at Senior High SchoolHelping My ParentsA Memorable TripMy English PresentationA Rainy MorningJoining a School EventA Funny Moment in ClassHelping with Seaweed WorkMy Weekend ActivityAn Unforgettable Day
Length:
Beginner: 100–130 words
Intermediate: 140–180 words
Advanced: 200–250 words

C. Example Text — “Helping My Mother at Home”

Last Sunday, I helped my mother at home. It was a sunny morning, and I did not go to school because it was the weekend. After breakfast, my mother asked me to help her clean the house.

First, I swept the floor in the living room. Then, I cleaned the table and arranged the chairs. After that, I washed some dishes in the kitchen. I was a little tired, but I continued helping my mother.

When the house became clean, my mother smiled and thanked me. I felt happy because I could make her work easier. It was a simple experience, but it taught me to be more responsible at home.

B. Writing Template

Title My … Orientation Last …, I … It happened in/at … I was with … Events First, … Then, … After that, … Next, … Finally, … Reorientation I felt … because … It was a/an … experience. I learned that …

D. Self-Editing Checklist

  • I wrote a title.
  • I wrote the orientation.
  • I wrote the events in order.
  • I wrote the reorientation.
  • I used simple past tense.
  • I used time connectives.
  • I used correct punctuation.
  • I used capital letters correctly.
  • My story is clear.
  • I explained my feeling or lesson.
16
Presenting Section

Tell Your Personal Experience in Front of the Class

Duration: 1–2 minutes.

Presentation Frame Good morning, everyone. Today, I want to tell you about my experience. Last …, I … First, … Then, … After that, … Finally, … I felt … It was a/an … experience. Thank you.

Speaking Checklist

ChecklistYes/No
I speak clearly.
I use past tense.
I use time connectives.
I do not speak too fast.
I look at my friends.
I speak with confidence.
17
Final Task — No Answer Key

Read, Write, and Speak

Task 1 — Reading: “A Busy Day at School”

Last Friday, my class had a cleaning activity at school. We came earlier than usual because our teacher asked us to clean the classroom before the lesson started.

First, we moved the chairs and tables. Then, some students swept the floor while others cleaned the windows. After that, we arranged the books and decorated the class wall with simple posters.

The activity was tiring, but we worked together happily. When the classroom looked clean and bright, we felt proud. I learned that teamwork could make difficult work easier.

  1. When did the activity happen?
  2. Why did the students come earlier than usual?
  3. What did they do first?
  4. What did they do after sweeping the floor?
  5. How did they feel at the end?
  6. What lesson did the writer learn?

Task 2 — Writing

Write a personal recount text about your own experience.

  • title;
  • orientation;
  • series of events;
  • reorientation;
  • simple past tense;
  • at least five time connectives;
  • at least ten past verbs;
  • your feeling or lesson.

Task 3 — Speaking

Present your recount text in front of the class.

  • introduce your experience;
  • tell events in order;
  • use past tense;
  • use clear voice;
  • use time connectives;
  • explain your feeling at the end.
18
Assessment Rubric

Writing and Speaking Rubrics

A. Writing Rubric

AspectExcellentGoodNeeds Support
StructureOrientation, events, and reorientation are clearStructure is mostly clearStructure is confusing
ChronologyEvents are in logical orderSome events are clearEvents are not organized
GrammarSimple past mostly accurateSome grammar errorsMany grammar errors
VocabularyUses varied verbs and feelingsUses basic vocabularyVery limited vocabulary
MechanicsGood punctuation and capitalizationSome mistakesMany mistakes
Personal ReflectionFeeling/lesson is clearFeeling is simpleNo clear reflection

B. Speaking Rubric

AspectExcellentGoodNeeds Support
ContentExperience is clear and completeExperience is understandableStory is too short/confusing
Past TenseUses past verbs accuratelySome errorsMany errors
SequenceUses time connectives wellUses some connectivesNo clear sequence
PronunciationClear and easy to understandSome unclear wordsDifficult to understand
ConfidenceConfident and naturalFairly confidentVery hesitant
Non-verbalGood eye contact and postureSome eye contactReads fully/no eye contact
19
Teacher Notes & Chapter Summary

Scaffold First, Then Build Independence

Teacher Notes

  • Mulai dari pengalaman yang dekat dengan kehidupan siswa.
  • Jangan langsung meminta teks panjang. Mulai dari verb list, sentence frame, 5 sentences, short paragraph, full recount text.
  • Gunakan gambar berseri agar siswa memahami urutan kejadian.
  • Latih verb 1 → verb 2 secara rutin.
  • Siswa boleh memakai pengalaman lokal seperti membantu rumput laut, berjalan ke sekolah saat rob, menunggu kapal, ikut memancing, kegiatan upacara, lomba kelas, perjalanan ke Makassar atau Pangkep.
  • Untuk speaking, mulai dari pair work sebelum presentasi kelas.
  • Fokus awal bukan grammar sempurna, tetapi keberanian menyusun cerita dalam urutan yang jelas.
SkillWhat Students Learn
ListeningUnderstand personal recount stories
SpeakingTell past experiences orally
ReadingIdentify orientation, events, and reorientation
ViewingArrange picture story into past events
WritingWrite a personal recount text
PresentingPresent a personal experience
GrammarSimple past tense, was/were, past verbs
VocabularyActivities, feelings, time connectives
Digital LiteracyCaption/story post about past experiences
Full Original Material Text — preserved for completeness
CHAPTER 2
PERSONAL RECOUNT TEXT
Telling Personal Experiences
Tema: Menceritakan pengalaman pribadi
Kelas: X / Fase E
Level siswa: Beginner–Elementary
Aplikasi: IsleLearn
Output akhir: Siswa menulis dan menceritakan pengalaman pribadi dalam bentuk personal recount text.
1. Situational Hook
Setiap siswa pasti punya pengalaman yang bisa diceritakan.
Mungkin kamu pernah:

terlambat ke sekolah karena hujan atau rob;
ikut orang tua memasang tali rumput laut;
pergi memancing pada malam hari;
mengikuti lomba antar kelas;
naik kapal ke Makassar;
membantu keluarga di rumah;
mengalami hari pertama masuk SMA;
menghadiri acara sekolah;
mengalami kejadian lucu, menegangkan, atau berkesan.
Dalam bahasa Inggris, pengalaman seperti itu bisa ditulis dalam bentuk personal recount text.
Contoh pertanyaan sederhana:

What happened to you yesterday?
Where did you go last holiday?
What did you do last weekend?
What was your most memorable experience?
2. Learning Objectives
Setelah mempelajari bab ini, siswa mampu:
memahami isi teks recount sederhana tentang pengalaman pribadi;
mengidentifikasi urutan kejadian dalam teks recount;
menemukan informasi penting: siapa, kapan, di mana, apa yang terjadi, dan bagaimana perasaan penulis;
menggunakan simple past tense untuk menceritakan pengalaman masa lalu;
menggunakan time connectives seperti first, then, after that, finally;
menggunakan action verbs seperti went, helped, saw, joined, visited;
menceritakan pengalaman pribadi secara lisan;
menulis personal recount text dengan struktur yang benar;
memperbaiki tulisan sendiri melalui checklist;
mempresentasikan pengalaman pribadi secara singkat dan jelas.
3. What Is Personal Recount Text?
Personal recount text adalah teks yang menceritakan pengalaman pribadi penulis yang terjadi di masa lalu.
Tujuan utamanya adalah:

to retell past experiences in chronological order.
Artinya: menceritakan kembali pengalaman masa lalu berdasarkan urutan waktu.
Examples of Personal Recount Topics
Topic	Example
School experience	My First Day at Senior High School
Family experience	Helping My Mother at Home
Island life experience	Helping My Father with Seaweed
Travel experience	My Trip to Makassar
Funny experience	A Funny Moment in Class
Unforgettable experience	The Day I Got Caught in the Rain
Competition experience	Joining a Class Competition
Daily life experience	A Busy Sunday Morning
4. Social Function
Personal recount text digunakan untuk:
menceritakan pengalaman pribadi;
berbagi cerita dengan orang lain;
menyampaikan perasaan tentang kejadian masa lalu;
melatih kemampuan menyusun cerita berdasarkan urutan waktu;
membuat pembaca memahami apa yang terjadi pada penulis.
Contoh dalam kehidupan nyata:
Situation	Recount Use
Menulis diary	Menceritakan kejadian hari itu
Bercerita kepada teman	Menceritakan pengalaman lucu atau menarik
Tugas Bahasa Inggris	Menulis pengalaman liburan
Caption media sosial	Menceritakan kegiatan yang sudah terjadi
Presentasi kelas	Menceritakan pengalaman berkesan
5. Generic Structure of Recount Text
Personal recount text biasanya memiliki tiga bagian utama.
Structure	Function	Example
Orientation	Mengenalkan siapa, kapan, dan di mana kejadian terjadi	Last Sunday, I went to the beach with my friends.
Series of Events	Menceritakan kejadian secara berurutan	First, we walked to the beach. Then, we played near the water.
Reorientation	Menutup cerita dengan perasaan atau kesan pribadi	It was a simple but unforgettable day.
Simple Formula
Orientation:
Who? When? Where?
Events:
What happened first?
What happened next?
What happened after that?

Reorientation:
How did you feel?
What did you learn?
Was it memorable?

6. Language Features
A. Simple Past Tense
Recount text menggunakan simple past tense karena menceritakan kejadian yang sudah terjadi.
Pattern 1: Verb 2
Subject + Verb 2 + Object/Complement
Examples:
I went to school early.
We played football after class.
She helped her mother.
They visited their grandmother.
He caught a fish.
Pattern 2: Was / Were
Subject	Past To Be	Example
I / He / She / It	was	I was happy.
You / We / They	were	We were tired.
Examples:
I was nervous on my first day.
The weather was hot.
We were very tired.
My friends were excited.
B. Regular and Irregular Verbs
Regular Verbs
Regular verbs biasanya ditambah -ed.
Verb 1	Verb 2	Meaning
play	played	bermain
help	helped	membantu
visit	visited	mengunjungi
watch	watched	menonton
clean	cleaned	membersihkan
join	joined	mengikuti
walk	walked	berjalan
arrive	arrived	tiba
start	started	memulai
finish	finished	menyelesaikan
Irregular Verbs
Irregular verbs berubah bentuk.
Verb 1	Verb 2	Meaning
go	went	pergi
come	came	datang
see	saw	melihat
eat	ate	makan
drink	drank	minum
buy	bought	membeli
bring	brought	membawa
take	took	mengambil/membawa
make	made	membuat
meet	met	bertemu
get	got	mendapatkan
catch	caught	menangkap
find	found	menemukan
feel	felt	merasa
hear	heard	mendengar
say	said	mengatakan
sit	sat	duduk
run	ran	berlari
swim	swam	berenang
write	wrote	menulis
C. Time Connectives
Time connectives digunakan untuk menyusun urutan kejadian.
Time Connective	Meaning	Example
yesterday	kemarin	Yesterday, I helped my mother.
last night	tadi malam	Last night, I studied English.
last Sunday	hari Minggu lalu	Last Sunday, I went fishing.
last holiday	liburan lalu	Last holiday, I visited Makassar.
first	pertama	First, we prepared our bags.
then	kemudian	Then, we walked to school.
after that	setelah itu	After that, we joined the class.
next	berikutnya	Next, we cleaned the classroom.
finally	akhirnya	Finally, we went home.
in the morning	pada pagi hari	In the morning, I woke up early.
in the afternoon	pada sore hari	In the afternoon, I helped my father.
at night	pada malam hari	At night, I did my homework.
D. Personal Pronouns
Personal recount biasanya menggunakan kata ganti orang pertama.
Pronoun	Use
I	saya
We	kami/kita
My	milik saya
Our	milik kami/kita
Me	saya sebagai objek
Us	kami/kita sebagai objek
Examples:
I went to the beach.
We helped our parents.
My father prepared the boat.
The teacher asked us to clean the class.
E. Adverbs of Manner
Adverbs of manner menjelaskan bagaimana suatu aksi dilakukan.
Adverb	Meaning	Example
slowly	pelan-pelan	We walked slowly.
carefully	dengan hati-hati	I carried the books carefully.
happily	dengan gembira	We played happily.
quickly	dengan cepat	He ran quickly.
quietly	dengan tenang	We listened quietly.
loudly	dengan keras	They laughed loudly.
seriously	dengan serius	I studied seriously.
patiently	dengan sabar	She waited patiently.
safely	dengan aman	We arrived safely.
7. Vocabulary Bank
A. School Activities
English	Indonesian
attended the class	mengikuti pelajaran
joined the ceremony	mengikuti upacara
cleaned the classroom	membersihkan kelas
submitted homework	mengumpulkan PR
answered questions	menjawab pertanyaan
studied together	belajar bersama
joined a competition	mengikuti lomba
practiced English	berlatih bahasa Inggris
presented a story	mempresentasikan cerita
helped a friend	membantu teman
B. Daily Activities
English	Indonesian
woke up	bangun
took a bath	mandi
had breakfast	sarapan
went to school	pergi ke sekolah
helped my mother	membantu ibu
helped my father	membantu ayah
cleaned the house	membersihkan rumah
washed clothes	mencuci pakaian
cooked rice	memasak nasi
studied at night	belajar malam
C. Island Life Activities
English	Indonesian
went fishing	pergi memancing
joined my father on the boat	ikut ayah di perahu
helped tie seaweed seedlings	membantu mengikat bibit rumput laut
harvested seaweed	memanen rumput laut
dried seaweed	menjemur rumput laut
carried a basket	membawa keranjang
waited for the boat	menunggu kapal
walked through seawater	berjalan melewati air laut
watched the waves	melihat ombak
returned home late	pulang terlambat
D. Feelings
English	Indonesian
happy	senang
sad	sedih
tired	lelah
nervous	gugup
excited	bersemangat
worried	khawatir
afraid	takut
proud	bangga
surprised	terkejut
thankful	bersyukur
bored	bosan
confused	bingung
relieved	lega
embarrassed	malu
amazed	kagum
E. Useful Sentence Starters
Function	Sentence Starter
Starting the story	Last Sunday, I ...
Giving place	It happened in ...
Giving time	In the morning, ...
First event	First, I ...
Next event	Then, I ...
More event	After that, I ...
Final event	Finally, I ...
Feeling	I felt ...
Closing	It was a ... experience.
Lesson	I learned that ...
8. Listening Section
Listening Text 1
“My First Day at Senior High School”
Last year, I had my first day at senior high school. I woke up earlier than usual because I felt nervous. After taking a bath, I wore my new uniform and prepared my books. My mother reminded me to be brave and polite.
When I arrived at school, I saw many new students in the yard. Some of them talked with their friends, while others stood quietly. At first, I felt shy because I did not know many people. Then, one student smiled and asked my name. We talked for a few minutes and walked to the classroom together.

The teacher welcomed us warmly. She explained the school rules and asked us to introduce ourselves. I was nervous when I stood in front of the class, but I tried to speak clearly. After school, I felt relieved and happy. My first day was not scary. It was memorable.

Listening Tasks
A. Main Idea
What is the text mainly about?
B. Details

When did the experience happen?
Why did the writer wake up earlier than usual?
What did the writer see in the school yard?
Who helped the writer feel less shy?
How did the writer feel after school?
C. Inference
What does the sentence “My first day was not scary” mean?

Listening Text 2
“Helping My Father with Seaweed”
Last Saturday afternoon, I helped my father with seaweed near the beach. The weather was hot, but the wind was fresh. My father brought several ropes, and I carried a small basket of seaweed seedlings.
First, we sat near the shore and prepared the ropes. Then, my father showed me how to tie the seedlings carefully. At first, I did it slowly because I was afraid of making mistakes. After some practice, I became more confident.

We worked for almost two hours. My hands felt tired, but I was happy because I could help my family. When we finished, my father smiled and thanked me. That afternoon, I learned that simple work can teach responsibility.

Listening Tasks
What did the writer do last Saturday afternoon?
Where did the activity happen?
What did the father bring?
Why did the writer work slowly at first?
What lesson did the writer learn?
9. Speaking Section
A. Asking About Past Experiences
Question	Answer
What did you do yesterday?	I helped my mother at home.
Where did you go last Sunday?	I went to the beach.
Who did you go with?	I went with my friends.
What happened after that?	After that, we played football.
How did you feel?	I felt happy and tired.
Was it memorable?	Yes, it was very memorable.
B. Dialogue Practice
Dialogue 1 — A School Experience
Alya: What did you do yesterday?
Rafi: I joined an English practice in class.
Alya: What did you do there?
Rafi: First, we listened to a short dialogue. Then, we practiced speaking with a partner.
Alya: Was it difficult?
Rafi: Yes, a little. I was nervous at first.
Alya: How did you feel after the practice?
Rafi: I felt happy because I could speak better than before.
Dialogue 2 — An Island Experience
Dina: Did you help your parents last weekend?
Salim: Yes, I did. I helped my father prepare the seaweed ropes.
Dina: Where did you do it?
Salim: We did it near the beach.
Dina: Was it tiring?
Salim: Yes, it was tiring, but I was proud.
Dina: Why?
Salim: Because I helped my family and learned something useful.
C. Speaking Mini Task
Tell your partner about one experience.
Use this frame:

Last …, I …
I went there with …
First, …
Then, …
After that, …
Finally, …
I felt … because …
Topics:
My first day at SMA
Helping my parents
A trip to the beach
A funny moment in class
A day when I was late
My weekend activity
Joining a school event
Helping with seaweed work
Going fishing
Visiting family
10. Reading Section
Reading Text 1 — Short
“A Rainy Morning”
Yesterday morning, I went to school in the rain. I woke up late because I forgot to set my alarm. I took a bath quickly and wore my uniform. My mother told me to bring an umbrella, but I forgot it.
On the way to school, the rain became heavier. My shoes were wet, and my bag was a little dirty. When I arrived at school, the first lesson had already started. I felt embarrassed, but my teacher allowed me to enter the class.

It was not a good morning, but I learned to prepare my things before sleeping.

Questions
When did the experience happen?
Why did the writer wake up late?
What did the writer forget to bring?
How did the writer feel when arriving at school?
What lesson did the writer learn?
Reading Text 2 — Medium
“My First English Presentation”
Last month, I had my first English presentation in class. The topic was “My Favorite Place.” I chose the beach near my house because it was easy for me to describe.
Before the presentation, I felt very nervous. I wrote my text at home and practiced it several times. My sister listened to me and helped me correct some words. On the presentation day, I stood in front of the class with shaking hands.

First, I greeted my friends. Then, I introduced my topic. I described the beach, the blue water, the small boats, and the fresh wind. I made some pronunciation mistakes, but my friends listened carefully. My teacher smiled and gave me helpful feedback.

After the presentation, I felt proud. I realized that speaking English was difficult, but practice made it easier. It was a simple presentation, but it gave me more confidence.

Questions
What was the presentation topic?
Why did the writer choose the beach?
Who helped the writer practice?
What did the writer describe?
What did the writer realize after the presentation?
Mention three past verbs from the text.
Reading Text 3 — Longer
“A Memorable Trip to Makassar”
Last holiday, my family and I went to Makassar. It was my first trip to the city after several months on the island. I felt excited because I wanted to see tall buildings, busy roads, and large stores.
We left Sapuka by boat early in the morning. The sea was calm at first, but after two hours, the waves became bigger. I felt a little afraid, so I sat near my mother and held my bag tightly. My father told me to stay calm. After several hours, we arrived safely at the harbor.

In Makassar, we visited my uncle’s house. The neighborhood was very different from my village. There were many vehicles, bright lights, and crowded streets. The next day, my uncle took us to a bookstore. I was amazed because I saw many English books, dictionaries, and school supplies. I bought one small notebook and a pen.

On the last day, we visited Losari Beach. The place was crowded, but the view was beautiful. We took some photos and ate traditional snacks. I felt happy, but I also missed the quiet atmosphere of Sapuka.

The trip taught me that every place has its own beauty. Makassar was busy and modern, while Sapuka was quiet and peaceful. I was thankful because I could experience both places.

Questions
When did the writer go to Makassar?
How did the writer travel from Sapuka?
Why did the writer feel afraid on the boat?
What did the writer see in the bookstore?
What place did the family visit on the last day?
How did the writer compare Makassar and Sapuka?
What lesson did the writer learn?
11. Viewing Section
Picture-Based Recount Activity
Guru dapat menggunakan gambar berseri, foto kegiatan sekolah, atau foto aktivitas sehari-hari.
Contoh gambar:

siswa bangun pagi;
siswa pergi ke sekolah;
siswa belajar di kelas;
siswa membantu orang tua;
siswa belajar di malam hari.
Viewing Questions
What happened first?
What happened next?
What did the student do after school?
How did the student feel?
What sentence can describe the last picture?
Sentence Frames
First, he/she …
Then, he/she …
After that, he/she …
In the afternoon, he/she …
At night, he/she …
Finally, he/she felt …
12. Grammar Lab
A. Change the Verbs into Past Form
go →
help →
see →
play →
eat →
bring →
study →
walk →
feel →
write →
Answers:
went
helped
saw
played
ate
brought
studied
walked
felt
wrote
B. Complete the Sentences
Use the correct past verb.
Yesterday, I ___ to school early.
Last night, we ___ English together.
My father ___ a fishing net.
I ___ very nervous before the presentation.
They ___ football after class.
Word bank:
played — studied — went — prepared — felt
Answers:
went
studied
prepared
felt
played
C. Arrange the Events
Arrange the sentences into a good recount paragraph.
Finally, we went home happily.
Last Sunday, my friends and I went to the beach.
After that, we sat under a tree and ate snacks.
First, we walked from our houses to the beach.
Then, we played near the water.
Correct order:
2 → 4 → 5 → 3 → 1

13. Common Mistakes
Mistake	Correction	Explanation
Yesterday I go to school.	Yesterday I went to school.	Recount memakai Verb 2.
I was go to the beach.	I went to the beach.	Jangan gabung was + Verb 1.
We was happy.	We were happy.	We memakai were.
She help her mother yesterday.	She helped her mother yesterday.	Help menjadi helped.
I feel nervous last week.	I felt nervous last week.	Feel menjadi felt.
Then I am happy.	Then I was happy.	Am menjadi was.
14. Guided Practice
Practice 1 — Complete the Recount
Complete the text with suitable words.
Last Sunday, I ___ my mother at home. First, I cleaned the ___. Then, I washed the ___. After that, I helped her cook rice. I felt ___ because I could help my family.
Word bank:
dishes — happy — helped — kitchen
Practice 2 — Write Sentences from Pictures
Look at the picture story and write five sentences.
Use:

first
then
after that
finally
felt
Example:
First, the student woke up early.
Then, he went to school.
After that, he helped his father.
Finally, he studied at night.
He felt tired but happy.
Practice 3 — Expand the Sentence
Make the sentence longer.
I went to school.
→ Yesterday morning, I went to school early with my friend.
I helped my father.
→ Last Saturday afternoon, I helped my father tie seaweed ropes near the beach.
I was happy.
→ I was happy because I finished my first English presentation.
We played football.
→ After class, we played football in the school yard for one hour.
15. Interactive Quiz
Question 1
What is the purpose of personal recount text?
A. To describe a person
B. To tell past experiences
C. To explain a process
D. To give instructions

Answer: B

Question 2
Which tense is commonly used in recount text?
A. Simple present tense
B. Simple past tense
C. Future tense
D. Present continuous tense

Answer: B

Question 3
Which one is a time connective?
A. Beautiful
B. Then
C. Strong
D. Tall

Answer: B

Question 4
Which sentence is correct?
A. Yesterday, I go to school.
B. Yesterday, I went to school.
C. Yesterday, I going to school.
D. Yesterday, I was go to school.

Answer: B

Question 5
Which part introduces who, when, and where?
A. Orientation
B. Events
C. Reorientation
D. Title

Answer: A

16. Writing Section
A. Writing Mission
Write a personal recount text about one experience.
Choose one topic:

My First Day at Senior High School
Helping My Parents
A Memorable Trip
My English Presentation
A Rainy Morning
Joining a School Event
A Funny Moment in Class
Helping with Seaweed Work
My Weekend Activity
An Unforgettable Day
Length:
Beginner: 100–130 words
Intermediate: 140–180 words
Advanced: 200–250 words
B. Writing Template
Title
My …
Orientation
Last …, I …
It happened in/at …
I was with …
Events
First, …
Then, …
After that, …
Next, …
Finally, …
Reorientation
I felt … because …
It was a/an … experience.
I learned that …
C. Example Text
“Helping My Mother at Home”
Last Sunday, I helped my mother at home. It was a sunny morning, and I did not go to school because it was the weekend. After breakfast, my mother asked me to help her clean the house.
First, I swept the floor in the living room. Then, I cleaned the table and arranged the chairs. After that, I washed some dishes in the kitchen. I was a little tired, but I continued helping my mother.

When the house became clean, my mother smiled and thanked me. I felt happy because I could make her work easier. It was a simple experience, but it taught me to be more responsible at home.

D. Self-Editing Checklist
Before submitting your writing, check:
Checklist	Yes/No
I wrote a title.	
I wrote the orientation.	
I wrote the events in order.	
I wrote the reorientation.	
I used simple past tense.	
I used time connectives.	
I used correct punctuation.	
I used capital letters correctly.	
My story is clear.	
I explained my feeling or lesson.	
17. Presenting Section
Presentation Mission
Tell your personal experience in front of the class.
Duration: 1–2 minutes.

Use this frame:

Good morning, everyone.
Today, I want to tell you about my experience.
Last …, I …
First, …
Then, …
After that, …
Finally, …
I felt …
It was a/an … experience.
Thank you.
Speaking Checklist
Checklist	Yes/No
I speak clearly.	
I use past tense.	
I use time connectives.	
I do not speak too fast.	
I look at my friends.	
I speak with confidence.	
18. Final Task — No Answer Key
Task 1 — Reading
Read the text and answer the questions.
“A Busy Day at School”
Last Friday, my class had a cleaning activity at school. We came earlier than usual because our teacher asked us to clean the classroom before the lesson started.
First, we moved the chairs and tables. Then, some students swept the floor while others cleaned the windows. After that, we arranged the books and decorated the class wall with simple posters.

The activity was tiring, but we worked together happily. When the classroom looked clean and bright, we felt proud. I learned that teamwork could make difficult work easier.

Questions:
When did the activity happen?
Why did the students come earlier than usual?
What did they do first?
What did they do after sweeping the floor?
How did they feel at the end?
What lesson did the writer learn?
Task 2 — Writing
Write a personal recount text about your own experience.
Your text must include:

title;
orientation;
series of events;
reorientation;
simple past tense;
at least five time connectives;
at least ten past verbs;
your feeling or lesson.
Task 3 — Speaking
Present your recount text in front of the class.
You must:

introduce your experience;
tell events in order;
use past tense;
use clear voice;
use time connectives;
explain your feeling at the end.
19. Assessment Rubric
A. Writing Rubric
Aspect	Excellent	Good	Needs Support
Structure	Orientation, events, and reorientation are clear	Structure is mostly clear	Structure is confusing
Chronology	Events are in logical order	Some events are clear	Events are not organized
Grammar	Simple past mostly accurate	Some grammar errors	Many grammar errors
Vocabulary	Uses varied verbs and feelings	Uses basic vocabulary	Very limited vocabulary
Mechanics	Good punctuation and capitalization	Some mistakes	Many mistakes
Personal Reflection	Feeling/lesson is clear	Feeling is simple	No clear reflection
B. Speaking Rubric
Aspect	Excellent	Good	Needs Support
Content	Experience is clear and complete	Experience is understandable	Story is too short/confusing
Past Tense	Uses past verbs accurately	Some errors	Many errors
Sequence	Uses time connectives well	Uses some connectives	No clear sequence
Pronunciation	Clear and easy to understand	Some unclear words	Difficult to understand
Confidence	Confident and natural	Fairly confident	Very hesitant
Non-verbal	Good eye contact and posture	Some eye contact	Reads fully/no eye contact
20. Teacher Notes
Untuk siswa beginner–elementary:
Mulai dari pengalaman yang dekat dengan kehidupan siswa.
Jangan langsung meminta teks panjang. Mulai dari:
verb list;
sentence frame;
5 sentences;
short paragraph;
full recount text.
Gunakan gambar berseri agar siswa memahami urutan kejadian.
Latih verb 1 → verb 2 secara rutin.
Siswa boleh memakai pengalaman lokal seperti:
membantu rumput laut;
berjalan ke sekolah saat rob;
menunggu kapal;
ikut memancing;
kegiatan upacara;
lomba kelas;
perjalanan ke Makassar atau Pangkep.
Untuk speaking, mulai dari pair work sebelum presentasi kelas.
Fokus awal bukan grammar sempurna, tetapi keberanian menyusun cerita dalam urutan yang jelas.
21. Summary of Chapter 2
Skill	What Students Learn
Listening	Understand personal recount stories
Speaking	Tell past experiences orally
Reading	Identify orientation, events, and reorientation
Viewing	Arrange picture story into past events
Writing	Write a personal recount text
Presenting	Present a personal experience
Grammar	Simple past tense, was/were, past verbs
Vocabulary	Activities, feelings, time connectives
Digital Literacy	Caption/story post about past experiences
Ini sudah menjadi draft materi Chapter 2 Personal Recount Text dalam bentuk biasa. Setelah ini bisa kita kembangkan menjadi versi HTML IsleLearn dengan gambar, TTS listening, quiz interaktif, writing box, picture story, dan desain modern seperti Chapter 1.
IsleLearn • Chapter 2 Personal Recount Text • English X / Fase E
Modern layout version with all original material preserved and images placed by section.
